metadata.title: DORA and NIS2 Regulatory Overlap Analysis
metadata.description: Mapping of overlapping and unique requirements between DORA (Digital Operational Resilience Act) and NIS2 (Network and Information Security Directive 2)
metadata.scope: Focus on ICT risk management, supply chain security, and incident reporting

governance_and_oversight.description: Management body responsibilities for cybersecurity risk management
shared_concepts: Management body approval of security policies
key_differences: DORA: Specific focus on digital operational resilience
NIS2: Broader cybersecurity risk management scope
DORA: Applies to financial entities only
NIS2: Applies to essential and important entities across 18 sectors

supply_chain_security.description: Third-party risk management and supply chain cybersecurity
dora_articles: Article 28 - General principles on key contractual elements
Article 29 - Preliminary assessment of ICT concentration risk at entity level
Article 30 - Key principles for ICT third-party service providers
nis2_articles: Article 21(2)(c) - Security in acquisition, development, and maintenance
supply_chain_security.assessment_approach: Use both DORA and NIS2 questionnaires for comprehensive supply chain assessment
key_differences: DORA: Focus on ICT concentration risk and exit strategies
DORA: Specific register of ICT third-party service providers
DORA: Contractual access and audit rights explicitly required
NIS2: Broader coverage of supply chain beyond ICT
NIS2: Secure development lifecycle requirements

incident_response_and_reporting.description: Incident detection, response, and regulatory notification
dora_articles: Article 19 - Classification of ICT-related incidents and cyber threats
shared_concepts: Root cause analysis and lessons learned
incident_response_and_reporting.assessment_approach: Combined incident management assessment focusing on both DORA and NIS2 timelines

dora_major_incidents.intermediate: Within 72 hours at the latest after initial notification
dora_major_incidents.final: Not later than 1 month from the intermediate report

key_differences: DORA: 4-hour initial notification (stricter)
NIS2: 24-hour early warning (less strict)
DORA: Specific classification criteria for major incidents
NIS2: Broader definition of significant incidents
DORA: Notifications to competent authorities and ESAs
NIS2: Notifications to CSIRTs and competent authorities

business_continuity_and_disaster_recover.description: Business continuity planning and ICT continuity
nis2_articles: Article 21(2)(b) - Business continuity, backup, disaster recovery
business_continuity_and_disaster_recover.assessment_approach: Combined BCP assessment covering both ICT-specific and broader business continuity
key_differences: DORA: Specific focus on ICT continuity
NIS2: Broader business continuity including crisis management
DORA: Impact tolerance and RTO/RPO for critical functions
NIS2: Emergency communication systems requirement

cybersecurity_risk_management.description: Risk assessment, control implementation, and ongoing risk management
nis2_articles: Article 21(1) - Risk analysis and information system security
key_differences: DORA: ICT-specific risk management
NIS2: Comprehensive cybersecurity risk management
DORA: Explicit digital operational resilience focus
NIS2: Proportionate measures based on entity size and risk

dora_articles: Article 9 - Protection and prevention (network security aspects)
network_security.assessment_approach: Network security architecture and monitoring assessment
key_differences: NIS2: Explicit network segmentation requirement
DORA: Focus on ICT system resilience

access_control_and_authentication.description: Identity and access management, multi-factor authentication
dora_articles: Article 9 - Protection and prevention (access control aspects)
nis2_articles: Article 21(2)(i) - Human resources security and access control
Article 21(2)(j) - Access control and asset management
key_differences: NIS2: Explicit MFA requirement (Article 21(2)(e))
NIS2: Secured communications requirement
DORA: Access control within ICT risk management framework

vulnerability_and_patch_management.description: Vulnerability scanning, patch management, and remediation
key_differences: NIS2: Explicit vulnerability disclosure requirement
DORA: Focus on ICT system vulnerabilities

cryptography.description: Encryption, key management, and cryptographic controls
shared_concepts: Data encryption (at rest and in transit)
cryptography.assessment_approach: Cryptographic controls and key management assessment
key_differences: NIS2: More explicit cryptography requirements
NIS2: Secured voice, video, text communications
DORA: Encryption within ICT protection measures

security_testing_and_assurance.description: Security testing, penetration testing, and control effectiveness
dora_articles: Article 25 - Testing of ICT tools and systems
Article 26 - Advanced testing of ICT tools, systems and processes based on TLPT
shared_concepts: Threat-led penetration testing (for DORA critical entities)
key_differences: DORA: Threat-led penetration testing (TLPT) for critical entities
DORA: Specific testing frequency requirements
NIS2: General security effectiveness assessment requirement

training_and_awareness.description: Security awareness training and cyber hygiene
key_differences: NIS2: Explicit management training requirement
NIS2: Basic cyber hygiene practices
DORA: Training aligned with ICT risk management

ict_concentration_risk.description: Assessment and management of concentration risk from ICT third-party dependencies
requirements: Identification of single points of failure
Assessment of concentration risk at entity level
ict_concentration_risk.why_unique: DORA is focused on preventing systemic risk in the financial sector from over-reliance on limited ICT service providers

ict_third_party_service_provider_exit_st.description: Detailed exit strategies and transition plans for critical ICT services
requirements: Documented exit strategies for all critical ICT third-party services
ict_third_party_service_provider_exit_st.why_unique: DORA mandates comprehensive exit planning to ensure operational continuity in financial sector

ict_third_party_register.description: Register of information on all ICT third-party service providers
requirements: Maintain register of all ICT third-party service providers
Make register available to competent authorities
ict_third_party_register.why_unique: DORA requires formal register for regulatory oversight of systemic ICT risk

threat_led_penetration_testing.description: Advanced testing framework for critical entities
requirements: Threat-led penetration testing at least every 3 years
threat_led_penetration_testing.why_unique: DORA mandates advanced, threat-based testing for financial entities due to high risk of targeted attacks

ict_related_incident_major_classificatio.description: Specific classification criteria for major ICT-related incidents
ict_related_incident_major_classificatio.why_unique: DORA has specific, quantitative criteria for major incident classification in financial sector

digital_operational_resilience_testing.description: Comprehensive testing program for ICT systems
digital_operational_resilience_testing.why_unique: DORA mandates comprehensive, structured testing program specific to digital operational resilience

multi_factor_authentication_mandatory.description: Explicit requirement for multi-factor authentication or continuous authentication
requirements: Secured voice, video, and text communications
multi_factor_authentication_mandatory.why_unique: NIS2 explicitly mandates MFA, whereas DORA includes it within broader access control requirements

vulnerability_disclosure.why_unique: NIS2 explicitly requires vulnerability disclosure programs, important for critical infrastructure

basic_cyber_hygiene.description: Basic cyber hygiene practices for all personnel
requirements: Basic cyber hygiene training for all staff
basic_cyber_hygiene.why_unique: NIS2 emphasizes foundational cyber hygiene across all sectors

secured_emergency_communications.description: Emergency communication systems that remain operational during crises
secured_emergency_communications.why_unique: NIS2 requires specific emergency communication capabilities for essential services continuity

physical_and_environmental_security.description: Physical security of personnel and facilities
physical_and_environmental_security.why_unique: NIS2 explicitly addresses physical security, important for critical infrastructure protection

cyber_threat_notification.description: Notification of significant cyber threats even without incidents
requirements: Report significant cyber threats to authorities
cyber_threat_notification.why_unique: NIS2 requires proactive threat sharing to enable coordinated defense across sectors

service_recipient_notification.description: Notification to customers when incidents affect services
requirements: Notify service recipients of incidents affecting them
Enable recipients to take protective measures
service_recipient_notification.why_unique: NIS2 mandates customer notification to enable ecosystem-wide incident response

proportionate_measures_based_on_entity_t.description: Proportionate cybersecurity measures based on entity classification
requirements: Appropriate to entity size and sector
Different requirements for essential vs. important entities
proportionate_measures_based_on_entity_t.why_unique: NIS2 has explicit proportionality requirement recognizing diverse entity sizes and sectors

combined_assessment_strategy.approach: Organizations subject to both DORA and NIS2 should conduct integrated compliance assessment

tasks: Identify if organization is subject to DORA (financial entity)
Identify if organization is subject to NIS2 (essential/important entity)
Map internal controls to both frameworks
Assess current state against DORA requirements
Assess current state against NIS2 requirements
Identify overlapping requirements
Identify unique requirements from each regulation
Implement common controls that satisfy both regulations
Implement DORA-specific requirements (e.g., TLPT, exit strategies)
Implement NIS2-specific requirements (e.g., MFA, physical security)
Map all controls to SCF for unified framework
Establish single governance body for digital resilience and cybersecurity
Create integrated risk management framework
Unified third-party risk management program
Combined incident response and reporting processes
Conduct testing that satisfies both DORA and NIS2 requirements
Unified security monitoring and detection
Integrated compliance monitoring and reporting
Maintain separate reporting to DORA and NIS2 authorities
Unified internal compliance dashboard
Regular compliance reviews covering both regulations

option_1_combined.description: Use combined DORA/NIS2 questionnaire for vendors subject to both
option_1_combined.when_to_use: When assessing ICT third-party providers serving financial entities that are also essential/important entities

option_2_targeted.description: Use separate DORA or NIS2 questionnaire based on primary regulatory driver
option_2_targeted.when_to_use: When one regulation is the primary driver and the other is secondary

option_3_modular.description: Start with common questions, add regulation-specific modules
option_3_modular.when_to_use: When flexibility is needed for diverse vendor population

dora.publication: Official Journal of the European Union, 14 December 2022

nis2.full_title: Directive (EU) 2022/2555 - Network and Information Security Directive 2
nis2.publication: Official Journal of the European Union, 27 December 2022
